finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_08.md:33; chapters/chapter_08.md:41; chapters/chapter_08.md:43; chapters/chapter_08.md:45; chapters/chapter_08.md:49; chapters/chapter_08.md:59; chapters/chapter_08.md:61; chapters/chapter_08.md:79; chapters/chapter_08.md:81; chapters/chapter_08.md:85
finding_results.notes: Four passages revised across the Marcus interrogation scene. (1) HVAC resumption at line 33 now fragments mid-list with a lip-wetting break. (2) Secondary cluster reveal at lines 41-49 is interrupted by a yawn that costs him the thread, a forearm seizure, and a dry heave; Noor prompts 'Not all of it' to keep the information moving. (3) UPS shutdown procedure at line 59 fragments mid-phrase ('batteries right there with--') with a cognitive stall; Noor now states 'And the dock is Lena's' at line 61, contributing knowledge rather than receiving a briefing. (4) Mechanical room overview at lines 79-85 is broken into short fragments ('Compressors. Air handlers.') with a cramp interrupting the lock-system explanation; Noor infers the tactical implication at line 81 (cluster + HVAC in one room = dual target) rather than Marcus delivering a complete tactical analysis. No multi-sentence technical briefing now passes without physical interruption, and Noor extracts the key operational conclusion from partial answers.

summary: Single finding addressed: Marcus's withdrawal-scene dialogue fragmented across four passages to eliminate clean sequential systems briefings. Physical interruptions (forearm seizure, dry heave, cramps, cognitive stalls) now break every technical disclosure. Noor contributes two operational conclusions she formerly received passively: the Lena territorial obstacle and the tactical value of the mechanical sublevel housing both compute and HVAC. All essential information (cluster location, ~30% compute share, UPS battery shutdown, separate lock system) remains legible. Setups (secondary_cluster), payoffs (marcus_addiction), family messages, cafeteria killing, and Oxy confession scenes are untouched.
